items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: Mmhmm, I see. You've done a great job—

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: Oh, {PlayerName} and Ms. Zani! Nice to see you again!

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: It's been a while. Mind if I ask what you were doing just now?

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: Oh, we're just chatting. They seemed lonely. Not many people talk to them.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: This train Echo? It's been running since the vault was built. It chugs along, doing its thing in silence.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: But I do believe your company has made its day.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: But if I remember correctly, you are not the representative the Order informed me of.

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: Yes, it was supposed to be another Acolyte. But she's busy with the Order's work, so I volunteered.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Huh. Did the thought of working with the Montellis scare her away?

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: Perhaps, Ms. Zani, but I'm sure she'd think differently if she met a Montelli employee like you.

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: Seeing as the Montelli family and the Order are both blessed by the Sentinel, our paths were bound to cross eventually.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: ...I'm glad you see it that way. Now, do you have it?

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: Certainly, as you requested, the escorts carried an empty case while the relic stayed with me.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Thank you.

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: It's an important relic entrusted to the Montellis. That's all I know.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Same here, but it doesn't matter. Lady Carlotta gave me this job. I just follow orders, no questions asked. That's how you work for the Montellis.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Our top priority is to get the job done, nice and clean.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: By the way, {PlayerName}, the device from Lady Carlotta should have top security clearance, so you'll be able to operate the train Echo. I hope you enjoy driving.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: The vault's security mode has been activated. Someone touched something they shouldn't have.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Unlikely. All personnel were sent home at the start of this assignment. The highest security protocols are in place too.

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: Should we ask for outside help?

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: We can't, not while the vault's security Echo is there. It's capable of sensing danger.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Once it detects a threat, it locks down the vault, jams all signals, and activates a bunch of troublesome Echo guards.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Ha, a triple whammy.

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: That means there's no way out and no hope for backup...

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Any backdoors? The Montellis must have designed one.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: There is, but it's a bit tricky. Anyway, we need to access the security room first. Your clearance might come in handy.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Don't worry. Keeping you safe is part of my job.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Alright, let's go.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: The security control room in this depository is right ahead. You can put your device to good use there.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Each depository has its own security room. Just verify your device here to obtain security clearance. It's only valid for this depository, though.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Necessary for the vault's security, but yes, it's a hassle.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: We're stuck here for now, and the only way out is to get clearance from each of the depositories. That means going deeper into the vault.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: How much deeper?

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Beats me. The security room only controls the depository it's in and grants access to the next.

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: So, aside from a handful of senior members of the Montelli family, no one knows the full layout of this vault?

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: I don't know. Maybe a handful of people who've worked here for over thirty years might.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Let's get clearance first. It might help us avoid most of the defense systems triggered by the lockdown.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: These Echo guards are controlled by the security system. You can also command them with your clearance.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: The higher your clearance is, the more obedient they become.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: More guards are gathering.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: The longer the vault stays in lockdown, the more defense systems and Echo guards get activated.

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: Someone's hiding over there!

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: It looks like a letter sent to his grandson. And this paper... was it torn from a book?

options.text: It must be from this book.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Hmm... I can't understand a word.

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: I can make out a little. It seems connected to cryptography.

items.speaker: Lily
items.text: It's an Echo book, young lady, and it seems fond of you. You think you're reading it, but it's actually the book communicating with you.

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: An Echo book? Incredible.

items.speaker: Lily
items.text: Yes. It doesn't speak, but it responds to the questions you ask. The more time you spend with it, the more you'll understand it.

items.speaker: Lily
items.text: Old Ash carried this book everywhere. I heard it even helped him design a coded lock for the vault.

items.speaker: Lily
items.text: That letter you saw is indeed a page from this book. I remember Ash tearing it out in a fit once. We all made him clean the bathroom as a punishment, hehe.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: So, Ash left this letter to tell his grandson about that mysterious "secret cache." Turns out it really does exist.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: He probably found out about the cache from the letter and is attempting to steal it.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Exactly. It's just like Acolyte Phoebe said. He triggered the lockdown to get to the depths of the vault without interference.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: If he'd acted like a professional and stuck to the plan instead of messing around, we wouldn't be in this mess.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: We need to find him before he puts himself or others in danger. Plus we need the security module to leave. Let's head to the lower control room and check the surveillance cameras.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Since this book belongs to the Montellis, I'll hold onto it for now.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Okay, let's sort out the clues.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: The lockdown is still in full effect, triggered when "Ash" removed the security module. To escape, we need to retrieve the module and use our clearance to reach the vault's depth.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: On top of that, thanks to him, we're stuck dealing with the defense systems.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: So, for both his sake and ours, we need to catch him as soon as possible.

options.text: How about we do it the other way around?
What if we approach this from another angle...
There must be a smarter way to go about this.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: He's running blind, getting more desperate as the lockdown escalates. But there's only one way he can go.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: And only one thing that can take him there.

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: The train Echo!

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Makes sense. He has no choice but to go to the train Echo's terminus. So...

options.text: We beat him to the vault's depth and wait.
We wait for him at the final stop.

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: Without us chasing him, he'll follow the main route and trigger fewer defense systems.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: That would save us a lot of trouble. I can help manage the security systems. With the clearance, I should be able to override some defenses and open shortcuts.

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: That should work. {PlayerName} and I will go retrieve the clearance. Ms. Lily, could you stay here and assist Ms. Zani?

items.speaker: Lily
items.text: Of course.

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: It's finished. We can get the clearance now.

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: I'm fine, really. It's just like hauling the Codex around back at the Order... But, uh, {PlayerName}, why are you staring at me like that? Is something wrong?

items.speaker: Zani (Contacting)
items.text: Stunning work, miss. If I hadn't seen this myself, I'd never believe your staff was meant to be used like that.

items.speaker: Lily (Contacting)
items.text: Yes. Very impressive.

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: Striking with an appropriate level of force clears the mind, enhancing one's purification.

items.speaker: Zani (Contacting)
items.text: Well, well... since when did our Acolyte start cracking jokes? Looks like {PlayerName}'s humor is rubbing off on you.

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: Uh... I wasn't joking.

items.speaker: Zani (Contacting)
items.text: Ahem. Maybe you should grab that clearance before they wake up.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Everything's under control now. The vault schematics show the next stop is the terminus. That man has nowhere to hide.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: But I'm not sure where the terminus is exactly. The Employee Manual only states that the Echo Depository is located at the vault's depth, without any further information about the station.

items.speaker: Lily
items.text: My memory is hazy... I do remember something dangerous out there, but that's all. I'm sorry.

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: It's alright, Ms. Lily. May the Sentinel guide our way.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Whatever it is, we have no choice but to move forward.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Right. Let's get this done quickly, {PlayerName}.

dialog.stepLabel: 落到特殊藏品区后对话
dialog.nodeDesc: 落到特殊藏品区后对话

items.text: The Echo train experienced an accident and fell, but fortunately, no one was injured. While the train Echo itself seems to have sustained only a minor scratch, it is now unable to move...

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: Phew... Is everyone alright?

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Thanks to the train Echo, we're still in one piece, though I have no idea where we are now.

options.text: There should be other train Echoes available, right?

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: We'll need it to get to the terminus.

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: Please wait... Ma'am, are you hurt?

options.text: She seems lost in her thoughts.

items.speaker: Lily
items.text: I'm fine, child. This place feels so familiar. It's like I've lived here for a long time.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Any details you can remember would be useful. Since employee training didn't touch on this area, we'll be going in blind in search of the exit.

items.speaker: Lily
items.text: Hmm... this place. I've been here before with many others, but their faces are a blur...

items.speaker: Lily
items.text: Yes, we rarely left this place, but I do remember taking the train somewhere very dark...

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Are you suggesting that people used to live in the vault?

items.speaker: Lily
items.text: Yes, if I'm not mistaken, a group of scholars made a home for themselves here.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: It seems the vault's structure is more complicated than we thought.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: I take it that this "very dark" place you mentioned is the terminus.

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: "Somewhere very dark"... What kind of place could that be?

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: We'll be just fine. Ms. Lily, take your time remembering. We'll take it slow.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Plus, we need time to find the exit.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Let's get going. We need to retrieve the stolen security module. That's our key to getting out.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Bad news. I know nothing about deciphering passwords.

items.speaker: Lily
items.text: You might want to try the Echo book. It's an expert at this sort of thing.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: It's nothing but riddles to me.

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: Ms. Zani, the book says the password is "0622," those numbers refer to...

items.speaker: Lily
items.text: The date when an innocent truthseeker was wrongfully convicted by fools. I remember, but the memories hurt.

items.speaker: Lily
items.text: They never hesitated to sacrifice for the truth. They shouldn't have had to carry such a burden.

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: Faith tells me that the truth you sought will eventually come to light, Ms. Lily.

items.speaker: Lily
items.text: Perhaps, but for now we must stay focused on what lies ahead.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Security lockdown… That's odd. The Employee Manual says this mode can only be activated by...

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Um, miss. If I may, what are you doing?

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: Me? Oh, this Echo seems to have a headache. I'm trying to help.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Wait, even if it is malfunctioning, you shouldn't hit it with your—
Beep... Bleep...

items.speaker: Echo Greeter
items.text: Depository One security module malfunction. Lockdown detected. Emergency pass override activated. Line 1 temporary access granted. Please evacuate via the nearest exit.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: What the?!

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: Is something wrong, Ms. Zani?

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: No... never mind.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: This is only speculation, but what if this "Ash" stole from the vault and took the security module? That would explain why a complete lockdown was triggered.

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: What's a hidden security module? Is it like a key?

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Pretty much. It's the monitoring core that powers this entire depository. If we don't get it back, we'll be stuck here for good.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: A vault employee could easily access the module, but nobody would dare steal it. Everyone knows that'll trigger a lockdown of the entire vault.

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: But what if that's exactly what they want?

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: Think about it. There's no one in the vault except for us, and all comms are blocked. It's the perfect opportunity for a thief.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Don't tell me they're searching for... Never mind, we need to catch this rat before they cause us more trouble.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Let's go find out.
...Hello.

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: Excuse me, ma'am, do you work here?
Sorry, if this sounds odd, but... I'm a bit confused. I don't remember why I'm here.

items.speaker: Lily
items.text: All I know is that I do work in the vault, and my name is... Lily.

items.speaker: Lily
items.text: But this isn't the section I oversee. Why am I here... Why are you here?

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Lady Carlotta has tasked us with a special delivery. May I see your ID, Ms. Lily?

items.speaker: Lily
items.text: I only have this card.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Thank you. Hmm... Confidential Researcher? The vault does have that position, but a researcher shouldn't be in this section.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: The ID is real—the anti-counterfeit features check out.

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: She doesn't look like she could be a thief's accomplice, either.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: That thing you're carrying seems to disagree.

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: The Order's relics are kept a secret from everyone, including an Acolyte like myself. I can't say what it's reacting to.

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: But... I do have a strange feeling about Ms. Lily.

options.text: We should bring her with us.

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: She could accidentally trigger something dangerous if left alone. That's the last thing we need.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Agreed. And if she is somehow involved, we'd save a lot of effort by taking her with us.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: I'm sorry, ma'am, but you'll need to come with us.

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: I understand your hesitation, but with the vault sealed, you'll be safer with us.

items.speaker: Lily
items.text: Alright, I'll come with you.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Good. Time to use your clearance, {PlayerName}. Let's find out what's really going on in this vault.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Ma'am, does the name "Ash" mean anything to you?

items.speaker: Lily
items.text: Old Ash? He used to say he wanted to sleep among the Echoes to study their behaviors. What a stubborn man.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: So the real Ash did exist. As for that man hiding over there... he might have stolen Ash's ID and gotten stuck here.

items.speaker: Lily
items.text: Ash's ID was stolen? Outsiders couldn't have done that—we've been in here for at least twenty years. Even with my fuzzy memory, I know that much.

options.text: Is this a vault or a prison?

items.speaker: Rover
items.text: Twenty years in this vault... Sounds like a living hell...

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: If it were me, I could maybe last... Well, ten years tops.

items.speaker: Phoebe
items.text: But... no one should be working in such awful conditions, Ms. Zani.

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Yeah, well, personally I just hope to live long enough to make the most out of my retirement someday. Anyway, we've got an imposter on our hands posing as "Ash."

items.speaker: Zani
items.text: Let's take the train to the Echo depository. We must find this thief and retrieve the security module. After that, we can decide what to do next.
